CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-20-2013 CONTINUED TO APR-24-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS THE FISHER FAMILY AND THIS DOES NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. COUNSELS WERE GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS AND NO ONE DID. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT OLD NAVY, LLC'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY THE ACTION - THE MOTION IS DENIED. THIS CASE RAISES NEGLIGENCE, PREMISES LIABILITY AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION & EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. DEFENDANT ADMITS THAT CA IS THE PRESUMPTIVELY CORRECT FORUM. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT CALIFORNIA IS AN INCONVENIENT FORUM. THE BALANCE OF PUBLIC AND PRIVATE INTERESTS DOES NOT SHOW THAT IT WOULD BE MORE JUST TO HAVE THIS CASE ADJUDICATED IN MICHIGAN. DEFENDANT IS HEADQUARTERED IN CALIFORNIA AND THEREFORE, A CALIFORNIA FORUM IS PRESUMPTIVELY CONVENIENT. (STANGVIK V. SHILEY, 54 CAL. 3D 744, 760). THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT (APPROVAL OF THE T-STANDS) ALLEGEDLY OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA. THE INSTANT CASE IS NOT COMPLEX AND WILL NOT UNDULY BURDEN THIS COURT'S CALENDAR. THE KEY WITNESSES (PLAINTIFFS) ARE WILLING TO RESOLVE THIS MATTER IN CALIFORNIA. LASTLY, ["T]HERE THUS MUST BE EVIDENCE-NOT MERELY BALD ASSERTIONS-TO SUPPORT THE TRIAL COURT'S DETERMINATION." (FORD MOTOR CO. V. INSURANCE CO. OF NORTH AMERICA (1995) 35 CAL.APP.4TH 604. HERE DEFENDANT MERELY RELIES ON THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT TO SUPPORT ITS MOTION. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT CLEARLY SHOW THAT CRUCIAL EVIDENCE OR WITNESSES ARE IN MICHIGAN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO CONFORM WITH CRC 3.1312) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Action For Minor Avery Mrozinski
Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Proceedings reported by: CAROL KAREN, CSR#8189.
Marc Lipton Esq., appearing for Plaintiff(s) Petitioner: Tod Mrozinski, Parent and guardian ad litem of Minor: Avery Mrozinski.
Petitioner Todd Mrozinski appearing via telephone conference by CourtCall.
After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $2,050,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $673,190.57 payable to Lipton Law Center, P.C.
Reimbursement for medical and all other expenses paid by the petitioner or the petitoners's attorney in the total amount of: $57,110.30
Other authorized disbursements payable directly to third parties in the total amount of: S69,562.94 to Health Partners, PO Box 1309, Minneapolis, MN 55444-1309, as Self Insured Erisa provider: $42,550.00
Optum Subrogation Services, 75 Remittance Drive, Suite 6019, Chicago, IL 60675-6019, on behalf of Abbott Laboratories, Self Insured Erisa Plan, $27,012.94.
Total allowance for fees and expenses from the settlement or judgment: $799,863.81

A net balance of $1,250,136.19 to be disbursed as follows:
For the purchase of three separate annuities for the benefit of Avery Mrozinski, as discribed in attachment 7c(2)(b) to order approving compromise.
Additional orders: The balance of the proceeds, which total less than $5,000.00, shall be distributed to Todd Mrozinski, to be held in trust for the use of Avery Mrozinski, his daughter.
Court signed Order Granting approval of Minors Compromise in open court.

Status Hearing Scheduled on May 15, 2014 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the purchase of three separate annuities. Counsel for Petitioner to file declaration providing the court with more information in reference to annuities. No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodge with Depart. 514 prior to Hearing.

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay The Action
Off Calendar Mar-07-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARC E. LIPTON OF THE LIPTON LAW, TEL. NO. (248) 557-1688 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ROB PHILLIPS, TEL. NO. 254-1212 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT GAP.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed that she knows the Fisher family and this does not affect her ability to be fair and impartial. Counsels were given the opportunity to ask questions and no one did. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OLD NAVY, LLC'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay The Action - The motion is denied. This case raises negligence, premises liability and Intentional Infliction & Emotional Distress. Defendant admits that CA is the presumptively correct forum. Defendant fails to show that California is an inconvenient forum. The balance of public and private interests does not show that it would be more just to have this case adjudicated in Michigan. Defendant is headquartered in California and therefore, a California forum is presumptively convenient. (Stangvik v. Shiley, 54 Cal. 3d 744, 760). The alleged wrongful conduct (approval of the T-stands) allegedly occurred in California. The instant case is not complex and will not unduly burden this Court's calendar. The key witnesses (plaintiffs) are willing to resolve this matter in California. Lastly, ["t]here thus must be evidence-not merely bald assertions-to support the trial court's determination." (Ford Motor Co. v. Insurance Co. of North America (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 604. Here defendant merely relies on the allegations of the amended complaint to support its motion. The amended complaint does not clearly show that crucial evidence or witnesses are in Michigan. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to conform with CRC 3.1312) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
